steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This Text Structure and Purpose question asks for the overall structure of the passage. The decisive clue is the final sentence: researchers "are hopeful that this discovery could aid in the development of new antibiotics for human use" — the passage pivots from what was found to where it points next, signaling a two-part structure: findings, then future research direction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — summarizes research findings and identifies an area for further research: Matches the passage exactly. The first two sentences report what the team discovered (ants detect and treat infected wounds; mortality reduced by 90%). The final sentence points toward future antibiotic development. ✓
  • Choice B — introduces the study and explains the research methods used: The passage never describes how the researchers conducted their work — no methods, procedures, or experimental design are mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — describes unique properties of the ants and speculates on how those properties evolved: The passage does not address evolution or how the ants' wound-treatment ability developed over time. ✗
  • Choice D — identifies an issue concerning the ants and proposes a solution: The ants' wound-treatment behavior is presented as a beneficial discovery, not as a problem. No issue affecting the ants is raised and no solution is proposed. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
